required_inputs.role: Respondent-item file for the 12 co-administered bridge items, validated against schema/wave_response.schema.json before distributions, factor models, linking, or correlations are estimated.
required_inputs.validation_rule: All rows must use wave_id anx_us_2026w07_cross_domain_bridge, benchmark_version v0.7.0, event_id no_event, and exactly the frozen somatic, economic, and epistemic item IDs listed in the preregistration.

required_inputs.role: Respondent-level analytic file containing eligibility, exclusions, weights, demographics, employment variables, information-environment variables, AI exposure, baseline anxiety, block order, item order, and paradata.
required_inputs.validation_rule: Each eligible respondent must join to 12 administered item rows unless item missingness is explicitly coded, and all preregistered exclusion variables must be present before outcome inspection.

required_inputs.role: Frozen item wording, response anchors, scoring keys, construct metadata, exclusion metadata, and validation metadata for the co-administered somatic, economic, and epistemic bridge item pools.
required_inputs.validation_rule: The analysis allowlist is exactly the 12 bridge item IDs, with somatic item_version v0.2.0 and economic and epistemic item_version v0.1.0.

packages.name: psych
packages.purpose: Polychoric correlations, ordinal EFA, corrected item-total correlations, omega summaries, and descriptive diagnostics.

packages.name: GPArotation
packages.purpose: Oblique exploratory rotations for cross-domain factor-structure diagnostics.

packages.name: lavaan
packages.purpose: Ordered-categorical CFA, correlated-factor models, bifactor sensitivity models, and multi-group invariance using WLSMV.

packages.name: semTools
packages.purpose: Ordinal reliability, measurement-invariance summaries, and CFA comparison tables.

packages.name: mirt
packages.purpose: Multidimensional graded-response IRT calibration, linking, item information, local dependence, and DIF sensitivity models.

packages.name: lordif
packages.purpose: Ordinal logistic DIF screening with practical effect-size diagnostics.

packages.name: survey
packages.purpose: Weighted descriptive distributions, weight diagnostics, and sensitivity estimates.

software_environment.version_lock_rule: Analyses must archive sessionInfo output and package versions exactly; any substitution requires a preregistered addendum showing unchanged confirmatory pass-fail decisions.

randomization_and_seeds.seed_policy: Seeds are fixed before outcome inspection, set immediately before stochastic procedures, and written to the bridge dossier with the exact R session lock.

sample_splits.overlap_rule: The bridge sample must be independent of prior Wave 5 and Wave 6 calibration respondents when panel identifiers or protected linkage evidence are available.

eligibility_rules.rule: Respondent is age 18 or older, resides in the United States, can complete the survey in English without assistance, and provided informed consent.
Respondents should not overlap with prior economic or epistemic calibration samples when protected panel linkage is available.

exclusion_rules.rule: Exclude respondents who fail the instructed-response attention check.
Exclude respondents who fail the check confirming that the survey asks about AI-related anxiety across bodily, work, and information scenarios.
Exclude respondents whose completion time is below one-third of the same-sample median after clear breakoffs are removed.
Exclude respondents who give the same substantive response to all 12 ANX items and also fail an attention or minimum-reading-time check.
Exclude respondents missing or giving non-substantive responses on more than 25 percent of administered ANX items.
Exclude confirmed duplicate respondents, non-consenting respondents, protocol-ineligible respondents, breakoffs without usable item data, vendor-confirmed fraud, and records unusable after audit.

non_exclusion_flags.rule: A single item response time below one-third of the sample median item time is retained as a sensitivity flag and does not exclude by itself.
Unemployment, career transition, high AI exposure, and high baseline general anxiety are retained for DIF or validity analyses and do not exclude by themselves.

weighting_policy.primary_estimation: Primary EFA, CFA, omega, IRT linking, DIF, invariance, and cross-domain correlation pass-fail decisions are unweighted unless the estimator has a validated survey-weight implementation.
weighting_policy.descriptive_estimation: Weighted and unweighted item distributions, missingness rates, sample characteristics, exclusion rates, and domain-block-order checks must all be reported.
weighting_policy.weight_construction: Construct post-stratification or raking weights using age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, Census region, employment status, occupation group, and stable AI exposure margins when available.

trimming.exception_rule: Weights outside 0.30 to 3.00 must be trimmed unless a documented sensitivity analysis shows a wider range is necessary and leaves bridge decisions unchanged.

missing_data_handling.item_distributions: Report observed, skipped, breakoff, technical failure, and quality-control missingness separately for each item before respondent exclusions and after analytic exclusions.
missing_data_handling.factor_models: EFA and CFA use observed ordered responses from analytically eligible respondents; pairwise polychoric correlations are allowed for EFA diagnostics, while CFA uses the estimator default for ordered categorical missingness without imputing ANX responses.
missing_data_handling.reliability: Ordinal omega and corrected item-total correlations are computed from eligible respondents with sufficient observed retained items; no ANX item response is imputed for pass-fail reliability gates.
missing_data_handling.irt: Graded-response IRT linking uses observed ordered item responses only; respondents with missing retained items contribute according to the package likelihood implementation and missingness must be reported.
missing_data_handling.external_validity: Cross-domain correlations and readiness models use complete observed retained item summaries; missing non-ANX covariates may use explicit missing categories for sensitivity only.
missing_data_handling.imputation_policy: Do not impute ANX item responses, item scores, latent bridge scores, exclusion flags, sample membership, or event fields; covariate-only handling cannot rescue a failed primary gate.

efa.estimator: Ordinal EFA on polychoric correlations with oblique rotation and parallel-analysis support across the 12-item bridge pool.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Retain item for confirmatory bridge modeling.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item for bridge failure or domain-specific revision.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No material secondary loading is detected.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require written construct interpretation and block simple aggregation.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Retain interpretable multidomain factor structure for CFA.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block cross-domain bridge readiness.

cfa.estimator: Ordered-categorical WLSMV CFA comparing correlated three-factor, unidimensional, bifactor, and second-order bridge models.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Correlated three-factor CFI meets strong bridge fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: If below 0.90 block bridge readiness; if 0.90 to 0.949 require caution.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Correlated three-factor RMSEA meets strong bridge fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: If above 0.080 block bridge readiness; if 0.061 to 0.080 require caution.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: All retained items meet loading threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block bridge readiness if fewer than three items remain in any domain.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Unidimensional model fits materially worse than the domain-stratified model.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require caution because domain separability is not established.

omega.estimator: Ordinal omega for each domain plus omega hierarchical and explained common variance for bifactor or second-order readiness review.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: All domains meet early reliability threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block cross-domain bridge readiness.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: General-factor reliability is sufficient for later overall-readiness review.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block overall ANX proposal while allowing domain-only bridge review.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: General factor explains enough common variance for later proposal review.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block overall ANX proposal.

irt.estimator: Multidimensional graded-response IRT linking with somatic anchor items and domain-specific candidate dimensions.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: All retained items show minimally useful discrimination.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag weak item for revision or bridge blocking.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Response categories function in ordered severity sequence.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block linking readiness if threshold disordering is unresolved.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No severe residual local dependence is detected.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block simple cross-domain linking or revise redundant items.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Somatic anchor drift is within bridge tolerance.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block using somatic items as stable cross-domain anchors.

dif.estimator: Ordinal logistic DIF with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control within domain families plus IRT DIF sensitivity where subgroup sizes permit.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No statistically flagged DIF remains after family control.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Evaluate practical DIF before bridge decision.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Practical DIF by pseudo R-squared is below threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block combined-score proposal unless immateriality is justified and disclosed.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Expected score impact is below material threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block or limit affected subgroup and aggregate claims.

invariance.estimator: Multi-group ordered-categorical CFA for configural, metric, and scalar or threshold invariance, with IRT linking fallback when CFA is unstable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Proceed to metric and scalar invariance comparisons.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block subgroup comparability claims for affected grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Metric invariance CFI threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag metric non-invariance for the grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar or threshold invariance CFI threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block mean-comparison and combined-score claims for affected grouping variable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar or threshold invariance RMSEA threshold passes.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block mean-comparison and combined-score claims for affected grouping variable.

external_validity.estimator: Latent cross-domain correlations, disattenuated construct correlations, and adjusted partial correlations after baseline general anxiety and AI exposure covariates.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Domains share enough common ANX variance for family-level readiness.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block overall ANX proposal because common-family evidence is too weak.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Domains remain sufficiently discriminable for stratified reporting.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block simple aggregation because discriminant validity is insufficient.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: ANX domains remain distinguishable from baseline general anxiety.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block overall ANX readiness unless incremental AI-specific variance is demonstrated.

validation_gates.fail_action: Label all bridge decisions underpowered and block readiness claims.

validation_gates.pass_action: Preserve v0.7.0 as bridge evidence only.
validation_gates.fail_action: Invalidate the packet because scoring was enabled prematurely.
Block event-study, trend, and capability-shock claims.

validation_gates.pass_action: Write one preregistered readiness label to the bridge dossier.
validation_gates.fail_action: Reject unregistered readiness labels and block aggregate claims.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Compare weighted and unweighted item distributions, missingness, and latent correlation summaries.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Primary unweighted pass-fail labels must be reported beside weighted descriptive estimates.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Estimate whether domain block order changes item thresholds, latent means, or cross-domain correlations.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Bridge conclusions must remain stable after including block order indicators.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Re-estimate cross-domain correlations after adjusting for baseline general anxiety and AI exposure.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Adjusted correlations should remain inside the preregistered 0.30 to 0.75 readiness band.

dossier_output.write_policy: The observed dossier must be written only after fielding and analysis; v0.7.0 remains a frozen candidate packet until a later evidence-bound review.

statistic_to_dossier_field.decision_use: Determines whether bridge pass-fail decisions have adequate sample size.
Blocks readiness when any co-administered domain lacks reliability.
Determines whether somatic anchor items remain stable in cross-domain administration.
Blocks overall readiness when domains do not share enough common ANX variance.
Blocks simple aggregation when domains are not sufficiently discriminable.
Records the only allowed bridge-readiness label and cannot authorize scoring by itself.
